zotero.abstractNote: We consider the problem of estimating f-divergences between two distributions P and Q given samples from each. While many estimators exist, they often suffer from high variance, lack of consistency, or require density estimation. We propose a new class of estimators based on the variational representation of f-divergences. By using a kernel-based approach to parameterize the variational functions, we obtain estimators that are consistent, computationally efficient, and perform well in practice. We provide a theoretical analysis of the convergence rates of our estimators and demonstrate their effectiveness on a range of tasks, including two-sample testing and training generative models.
zotero.conferenceName: Advances in Neural Information Processing Systems 32

extra.Editors: H. Wallach and H. Larochelle and A. Beygelzimer and F. d'Alche-Buc and E. Fox and R. Garnett
